Engagement Log

Experience

Teaching offensive fundamentals while keeping the reconnaissance-to-exploitation pipeline sharp.

Security Researcher & Mentor — Part Time
Hack To Live Academy
2024 — Present
  • Prepared 15+ students for the eJPT v2 certification by delivering structured lessons on enumeration, exploitation techniques, and professional reporting standards.
  • Designed lab-based learning tracks across TryHackMe and VulnHub, guiding learners through 30+ hands-on vulnerability assessment scenarios.
  • Developed a beginner curriculum on reconnaissance, privilege escalation, and post-exploitation that reduced average time-to-first-root by 50%.
Case Files

Hands-on projects

2024 — Present. Each file below is a self-directed engagement, lab-built and independently documented.

Web Application & API Security
Critical Surface
  • Assessed 20+ web applications and vulnerable lab environments, identifying OWASP Top 10 issues — XSS, SQL injection, race conditions, business logic flaws, file upload vulnerabilities, and auth bypass — with structured findings reports for each.
  • Completed the full PortSwigger Web Academy SQL injection track (blind, error-based, time-based), alongside DVWA and SQL Lab Master advanced scenarios.
  • Exploited OWASP API Top 10 issues — Broken Object Level Authorization, Mass Assignment, Excessive Data Exposure, JWT manipulation — across crAPI and vAPI, validating findings with Postman collections and Burp Suite interception.
Active Directory Attack Simulation
Full Domain Compromise

Built a multi-machine AD home lab from scratch and chained the following kill chain to full domain compromise:

Enumeration→ AS-REP Roasting→ Kerberoasting→ Silver/Golden Ticket
  • Mapped privilege-escalation paths with BloodHound; extracted credentials and performed lateral movement using Impacket and Mimikatz, documenting each stage in a kill-chain report.
  • Automated repetitive enumeration phases with custom Bash scripts, streamlining recon-to-exploitation across a multi-machine lab.
SIEM & Security Monitoring (Wazuh)
Detection Engineering
  • Deployed a Wazuh manager and enrolled Linux and Windows endpoints as monitored agents in a home lab.
  • Simulated brute-force, privilege escalation, and suspicious process events; correlated alerts and tuned detection rules to reduce false positives.
  • Practiced alert triage and incident response workflows, building familiarity with SOC-relevant log analysis pipelines.
General Lab Practice
35+ Root / System
  • Achieved full root/system compromise across 35+ privilege-escalation machines — including Kioptrix, DC: 1, DC: 2, ICA: 1, and Metasploitable — documenting attack paths and remediation recommendations for each.
Documented Solves

TryHackMe Write-ups

Room-by-room breakdowns of enumeration, exploitation, and privilege escalation — placeholder rows below, ready for your room names and links.

01
Academy
Anonymous FTP enumeration and hash cracking chained to a file-upload shell, then SSH brute-force for user pivot.
Read write-up → 02
Dev
Bolt CMS local file inclusion and NFS share mounting, with zip password cracking leading to SSH key access.
Read write-up → 03
Kenobi
NFS and SMB enumeration with FTP SITE command abuse to steal an SSH key, followed by PATH-hijack privilege escalation.
Read write-up → 04
Blackpearl
Subdomain fuzzing and CMS remote code execution chained to SUID binary abuse via GTFOBins for full root.
Read write-up →
